result.title: Test Complete
result.subtitle: Your Baseline Cognitive State
result.description:
This is your current cognitive activity baseline. Retest regularly to see the trend—
trends matter more than a single score.
result.adviceDefault: Consistent practice will lead to improvement.

adviceTips.logic: Do one logic puzzle a day (e.g., Sudoku or reasoning puzzles) to gradually improve analytical skills.
adviceTips.math: Don't reach for a calculator right away when doing mental math—try calculating it yourself first, even if it's slow.
adviceTips.vocab: Read 10 minutes of a physical book or long-form article daily, paying attention to word choices and expressions.

result.retestPrompt: Retest in 7 days to track your changes
result.retestDesc: Cognitive ability is like a muscle—measure regularly to see the trend. Your history is saved locally.
result.improved: Your cognitive activity has improved since last time ↑
result.worsened: The degradation index has increased since last time, keep an eye on the trend
result.unchanged: Same as last time, stay attentive
result.author: Hsiang Nianian

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Cognitive Rustproof · Project Origins
about.originP1: Since 2024, I've noticed myself increasingly throwing questions straight to AI instead of thinking them through first. My first reaction to a tough problem shifted from "let me think" to "let me ask." This shift is comfortable—but also unsettling.
about.originP2: "Cognitive Rustproof" isn't about denying the value of AI tools. It's about providing a simple way to regularly take a snapshot of your cognitive state. Not to prove anything, just to make the trend visible—because what you can't see, you won't care about.
about.usageP1: Complete {count} mixed-format questions (Logic / Mental Math / Vocabulary), each with a {seconds}-second time limit. After completion, you'll receive a cognitive activity score (0-100). The lower the score, the more active.
about.usageP2: A single test score has limited meaning—the real value lies in retesting. Leave at least 7 days between tests and connect the dots to see the trend.
about.privacyP1: We do not collect any personally identifiable information (no cookies, no user IDs). Each test result is stored anonymously and only used to generate platform-wide statistical distributions. Your personal history is saved in your browser locally and is not uploaded.
roadmapDoneItems: Personal trend line chart (view on stats page)
Challenge sharing—friends opening the link can see comparisons
Statistics grouped by AI usage
Light/dark theme toggle
Browser notification reminder (retest in 7 days)
PWA support (installable to desktop)
Dynamic OG share cards
44 questions in the bank, 20 randomly selected per test
Urgency prompt during the last 10 seconds of countdown
Test completion transition animation
Mid-test save/resume—pause and continue a 14-minute test
Multi-language support (中文 / English / 日本語)
Language switcher
Multi-dimensional cognitive radar chart
Home page "?" animation
Error Boundary global error handling
IRT adaptive assessment engine (EAP estimation + max Fisher selection)
Adaptive test coordinator (dimension rotation + theta tracking)
Difficulty calibration on all questions
Monolithic test-flow refactored into modular components
Fixed: Redis browser errors, hydration mismatch, language switch not updating questions
Phase 1 Week 3: LLM Item Generation—DeepSeek API + OpenAI SDK + Redis caching + rate limiting
roadmapLongItems: Phase 2: Micro-Intervention Engine—dual Agent (assessment + coaching) for personalized training
Phase 3: Cognitive Mirror—cross-dimensional analysis revealing blind spots
Phase 4: Cognitive Rustproof Economy—employer matching, insurance pricing, cognitive credit ecosystem

tier.cognitivePeakDesc: Your core cognitive abilities are in excellent shape. Even with frequent AI tool use, you maintain independent deep-thinking habits. You likely approach AI with critical thinking, treating it as a tool rather than a substitute.
tier.cognitivePeakAdvice: Keep up your current habits. You've proven that AI and independent thinking can coexist.
tier.mildDeclineDesc: Your cognitive abilities are generally good, but some early signs of decline are appearing. You may be starting to unconsciously rely on AI for certain types of thinking.
tier.mildDeclineAdvice: Before asking AI for help, give yourself 30 seconds to think independently. This small habit can effectively halt the decline.
tier.moderateDeclineDesc: Your cognitive activity has dropped noticeably. Daily over-reliance on AI for thinking tasks may be silently weakening your independent analytical skills.
tier.moderateDeclineAdvice: Set aside 15 minutes of AI-free deep reading/thinking time each day to let your brain get used to independent operation again.
tier.significantDeclineDesc: Significant cognitive decline detected. You may have grown accustomed to letting AI do the thinking that you should be doing yourself.
tier.significantDeclineAdvice: Take action now: 1) Reduce AI usage by 50% for one week 2) Do one logic puzzle daily without AI 3) Retest after one month.
tier.severeDeclineDesc: Your cognitive score is in the lowest range. Long-term reliance on AI to replace independent thinking has had a clear impact. Your brain is experiencing the "use it or lose it" effect.
tier.severeDeclineAdvice: Take this signal seriously. We recommend: 1) Start daily cognitive training immediately 2) Gradually extend AI-free thinking sessions 3) Develop a "think first, then ask AI" habit 4) Re-evaluate after one month.

lockedSelection: No changing your answer—after all, real fools don't get do-overs.
